claim: First end-to-end MCC measurement of the Layer 6 essentiality pipeline against Breuer 2019 labels. Run on the 4-gene reference panel (pgi, ptsG, ftsZ, JCVISYN3A_0305) with the real FastEventSimulator at scale=0.05, t_end=0.5s, ShortWindowDetector threshold=0.10. Result: MCC=0.333 (TP=1, FP=0, TN=1, FN=2). Below the brief's 0.59 target, expected for v0 - the short bio-time window doesn't let upstream-of-glycolysis transporter knockouts (ptsG) propagate.

secondary_run.note: Lower threshold tripped a false positive on ftsZ from dATP noise. Confirms thresholds need per-pool tuning, not a single global value.

source_detail: Predictions output: outputs/predictions_real_s0.05_t0.5_seed42_thr0.1.csv (gitignored). Metrics: outputs/metrics_real_s0.05_t0.5_seed42_thr0.1.json (committed). Reproducible via 'python scripts/run_full_sweep_real.py --reference-panel'.
caveats: n=4 only - reference panel for plumbing validation, not a full sweep.

MCC=0.333 is below the brief's >0.59 target. Achieving the target requires either (a) longer t_end_s so upstream KOs (e.g. transporters) propagate, (b) larger scale_factor so the noise floor drops, or (c) a smarter per-pool threshold based on a noise-floor calibration run over Breuer-non-essential genes.
Single-process runs at the v0 config: ~13s/gene wall. Full 458-gene sweep ~100 minutes. Practical path forward is multi-process partitioning of the gene list.
